response.justification: Strong match on distributed systems expertise (millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby), recent direct experience with AI-agent infrastructure and agentic workflows at re:cinq, and demonstrated staff-level technical leadership. However, the complete absence of Python is a stated must-have that creates a critical gap. The 6-month tenure in the most relevant recent role and ongoing career gap since October 2025 further temper confidence. Worth a conversation to assess Python capability and enthusiasm for the AI-driven development environment, but the must-have skill gap is a significant barrier.
strengths: Deep expertise in high-throughput distributed systems: handled millions of HTTP requests per second with microservices, async I/O, caching, and real-time databases at Dunnhumby.
Recent hands-on experience with agentic systems: directly contributed to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq, demonstrating comfort with AI-driven code production.
Strong architectural and design pattern mastery: multiple staff-level roles, proven software craftsmanship (TDD, trunk-based development), and open-source framework design (Fluent-Asserts, Trial).
concerns: No Python experience despite being explicitly listed as a must-have skill; resume shows strong TypeScript/JavaScript and DLang but no Python.
Brief tenure (6 months) in the most relevant recent role (re:cinq) and currently in a career gap since October 2025, raising questions about continuity and availability.
Limited demonstrated breadth of AI/ML product experience; AI exposure primarily limited to the recent re:cinq role rather than a consistent pattern across multiple positions.

key_factors.factor: High-throughput distributed systems experience at scale
Recent AI-agent infrastructure and agentic workflow experience
